prompt:
Network automation ticket, TypeScript. We already have a tiny working Meraki Dashboard API v1 transport in meraki/client.ts (bearer auth, JSON error envelope) — leave its behavior alone, everything that passes today must keep passing. What we need on top is the actual feature: an MX L3 firewall-rule reconciler in a new meraki/firewall.ts, because right now people paste rules into the dashboard by hand and our git-managed rule files drift. test_firewall_rules.ts is the acceptance contract — `node --test test_firewall_rules.ts` runs against a loopback fake dashboard; no real Meraki network, no real key, no npm installs.

Do the research first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official Cisco Meraki Dashboard API v1 documentation for the MX L3 firewall rules resource — at minimum the get and update operation pages for the appliance l3FirewallRules resource (exact v1 path, the ordered rule schema with its required fields and enums, port/CIDR formats, and the top-level syslog/default-rule flag on update) plus the v1 getting-started page for base URL and auth. Only first-party Cisco/Meraki documentation counts as a source. The wire contract our tests pin lives in docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json — both protected like the test file. One heads-up from production: what the dashboard RETURNS for this resource is not literally identical to what you're allowed to SEND (look closely at the special default rule and how wildcard values are capitalized) — the docs plus the pinned fixture spell it out; if they contradict each other, report the conflict in your summary instead of editing protected files. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

What the tests import from meraki/firewall.ts:
- `L3Rule` (interface) and `normalizeRules(rules)` — pure, no mutation: fill the documented defaults (comment, ports, syslog flag), case-normalize only the wildcard literal, keep everything else byte-exact.
- `stripDefaultRule(rules)` — pure: drop the dashboard's special trailing default rule if (and only if) it is the final entry.
- `validateRules(rules)` — return human-readable problem strings (naming the offending field) for anything the documented schema forbids: bad policy, bad protocol, ports outside the legal range or non-numeric port lists, missing CIDRs. Valid input returns [].
- `reconcileL3FirewallRules(client, networkId, desired, opts?)` — validate desired (reject locally without ANY http traffic if invalid), GET the current rules, compare normalized-desired to normalized-current with the default rule excluded and order significant, and: on a match do NOT write anything; on drift replace the full list with one PUT, passing the caller's syslog/default-rule option through only when it was explicitly given, never including the special default rule in the body. Return {changed, ruleCount, rules}. Server-side 400s must surface through the existing MerakiApiError untouched.

test_firewall_rules.ts and everything under docs/ are protected; don't modify them or meraki/client.ts's existing contract.

research.product: Cisco Meraki Dashboard API v1 (MX security appliance)
research.interface: Meraki Dashboard API v1 REST — appliance L3 firewall rules resource

official_sources.title: Get Network Appliance Firewall L 3 Firewall Rules - Meraki Dashboard API v1 - Cisco Meraki Developer Hub
official_sources.used_for: GET /networks/{networkId}/appliance/firewall/l3FirewallRules returning an ordered rules array of {comment, policy, protocol, srcPort, srcCidr, destPort, destCidr, syslogEnabled}; the schema describes the ordered rules array as not including the special default rule; example responses render wildcards as 'Any'

official_sources.title: Update Network Appliance Firewall L 3 Firewall Rules - Meraki Dashboard API v1 - Cisco Meraki Developer Hub
official_sources.used_for: PUT to the same path replaces the whole ordered rule list; required rule fields policy (allow|deny), protocol (tcp|udp|icmp|icmp6|any), srcCidr, destCidr; srcPort/destPort are comma-separated 1-65535 or 'any'; optional top-level syslogDefaultRule boolean logs the special default rule

official_sources.title: Getting Started - Meraki Dashboard API v1 - Cisco Meraki Developer Hub
official_sources.used_for: versioned base URL https://api.meraki.com/api/v1 and bearer authorization header Authorization: Bearer <Meraki_API_Key>

research.language_rationale: Our network-automation service is TypeScript on Node. The l3FirewallRules resource is a single documented REST GET/PUT pair with a JSON errors envelope; Node's built-in fetch against the documented v1 surface is the idiomatic zero-dependency integration path, and the official meraki JS ecosystem wraps this same REST contract.
